threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look for a seat on the bus instead of grabbing a pole like it’s a dance floor. You'll learn soon enough.
Last week on the 322, a guy serenaded the whole bus with classic soul tunes. Didn’t know we had a Brixton Idol in our midst!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing football in a backyard while a couple hosted a wine tasting next door. Berlin life in full swing.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can still find random parties in abandoned buildings here. Last weekend, an old factory turned club had a silent disco that felt like stepping back in time.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can navigate the Muni schedule chaos and still find time for tacos at La Taqueria, even during the delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni ride where a guy was singing opera while dodging the dog on the tracks. Classic San Francisco—both majestic and chaotic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all bars close at 1 AM, but the real spots stay open until 3 or 4, filled with locals discussing life over late-night vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still walk into bars asking for a 'café con leche' at 8 PM, totally oblivious to the evening vermut culture.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a Tube strike just means a longer wait for the Overground. Wait until you see the 'no trains' signs on every corner.
Caught a group debating the merits of Jollof rice vs. fried rice on the 59 last night. Their passion could fuel the bus all the way to Croydon.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard an improvised jam session from the courtyard, blending techno and tango. That’s when Neukölln felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it’s common to see people enjoying their coffee alongside their dogs, just chilling on the terrace. It's uniquely Berlin, and oddly heartwarming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to never trust a BART schedule, especially when the Civic Center station is a chaos zone. Delays are a way of life here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog casually trotting down the Civic Center tracks, while the Muni drivers just shrugged as if it’s an everyday occurrence.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think all the locals hang out at Plaza del Sol. Most of us prefer the small, quieter squares like Plaça de la Virreina for a real chat.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to use the shortcuts through the narrow alleys—always sticking to the main streets like tourists.
